project.name: PropManager Payment Workflow v2.1 - Enhancements
project.description: Enhancement features after v2.0 critical remediation. Includes multi-currency support, automated reconciliation, tiered billing, offline payment queue, data retention policy, frontend standardization, and cache optimization.

userDecisions.description: Enhancement decisions from comprehensive AskUserQuestion sessions

multi_currency.description: Support for multiple currencies (KES, USD, EUR, GBP)

billing.description: Tiered fee structure for platform fees

offline.description: Offline payment queue for poor connectivity

items.title: Add Currency Support to Payment Model and Migrations
items.description: Add currency field to payments table and related models. Support dynamic currencies from gateway providers.
steps: Create migration to add currency column to payments table (default: 'KES')
Add currency column to invoices table
Create app/Enums/Currency.php with KES, USD, EUR, GBP
Update Payment model with currency attribute and cast to enum
Update Invoice model similarly
Add exchange rate service integration (optional: Open Exchange Rates API)
acceptance_criteria: Currency stored on every payment and invoice
Enum provides label and symbol for each currency
Backward compatible (existing records default to KES)
items.verification: Create payment with USD currency; verify stored correctly.

rollback_plan.sql: ALTER TABLE payments DROP COLUMN currency; ALTER TABLE invoices DROP COLUMN currency

items.title: Update Paystack Integration for Multi-Currency
items.description: Paystack supports NGN, GHS, ZAR, USD, KES. Update integration to use invoice currency.
steps: Update PaystackService::initializeTransaction() to accept currency parameter
Convert currency to Paystack format (amount in minor units varies by currency)
Update frontend to display currency symbol correctly
Test with sandbox using USD currency
acceptance_criteria: Amount converted correctly to minor units
definition_of_done: Tests pass for KES and USD
items.verification: Create USD invoice; pay via Paystack; verify currency handled correctly.

items.title: Add Currency Selection UI for Landlords
items.description: Allow landlords to set default currency and supported currencies for their buildings.
steps: Add default_currency column to payment_configurations
Add supported_currencies JSON column to buildings
Create UI in Settings > Payment Methods for currency selection
Update invoice creation to use building's default currency
items.verification: Set building to USD; create invoice; verify USD is used.

items.title: Create Payment Reconciliation Service
items.description: Service to compare PropManager records with gateway records and identify discrepancies.
steps: Create app/Services/Reconciliation/PaymentReconciliationService.php
Implement reconcilePaystack(landlordId, dateRange): ReconciliationResult
Fetch transactions from Paystack API
Compare with local payments by reference
Identify: missing_locally, missing_remotely, amount_mismatch
Return structured result
items.verification: Create local payment not in Paystack; run reconciliation; verify flagged as missing_remotely.

items.title: Implement Automated Reconciliation Job with Alerts
items.description: Daily scheduled job to run reconciliation and alert on discrepancies >1 KES.
steps: Create app/Jobs/DailyPaymentReconciliation.php
Run reconciliation for all active landlords
Store results in reconciliation_reports table
Send email alert if discrepancies found
Schedule job to run daily at 2am
acceptance_criteria: Job runs daily for all landlords
items.verification: Manually run job; verify email sent for test discrepancy.

items.title: Implement Tiered Platform Fee Structure
items.description: Support different fee rates based on monthly volume. Higher volume = lower fees.
steps: Create app/Models/PlatformFeeTier.php with volume_min, volume_max, fee_percentage
Create migration for platform_fee_tiers table
Update BillingModelService to calculate fee based on MTD volume
Add tier display to landlord dashboard
acceptance_criteria: Fee percentage varies by monthly volume
Landlords see current tier on dashboard
Fee calculated correctly at tier boundaries
items.verification: Process payments to cross tier threshold; verify fee % changes.

items.title: Create Offline Payment Queue Table and Model
items.description: Store payment intents when tenant is offline. Process when connectivity restored.
steps: Create migration for queued_payment_intents table
Columns: id, tenant_id, invoice_id, landlord_id, amount, currency, payment_method, phone_number, status (pending/processing/completed/failed/expired), attempts, last_attempt_at, expires_at
Create app/Models/QueuedPaymentIntent.php
Add scopes: pending(), expired(), byTenant()
items.verification: Tinker: create queued intent, verify scopes work.

items.title: Implement Offline Payment Queue Processing Job
items.description: Scheduled job to process queued payment intents and send SMS confirmation.
steps: Create app/Jobs/ProcessQueuedPaymentIntents.php
Process pending intents not expired
Retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff
Send SMS via Africa's Talking on success/failure
Mark expired intents (>24 hours old)
Schedule job to run every minute
acceptance_criteria: SMS sent on success and failure
items.verification: Queue offline payment; wait for job; verify processed and SMS sent.

items.title: Implement 7-Year Data Retention Policy
items.description: Archive old payment data after 7 years per financial compliance requirements.
steps: Create migration for archived_payments table (same structure as payments)
Create app/Jobs/ArchiveOldPayments.php
Archive payments older than 7 years (created_at < now - 7 years)
Keep audit log of archived records
Schedule job to run monthly
Add archive query methods to Payment model
acceptance_criteria: Payments >7 years old moved to archive table
Original records deleted after successful archive
Archive can be queried if needed
items.verification: Create old payment (backdated 8 years); run job; verify archived.

items.title: Create Shared Payment Form Composable
items.description: Standardize payment form handling across all 10+ Vue payment components.
steps: Create resources/js/composables/usePaymentForm.ts
Include: form state, validation, loading states, error handling
Standardize payment method selection component
Create PaymentMethodSelector.vue shared component
Update existing payment components to use composable
acceptance_criteria: Composable handles common payment form logic
All payment components use shared composable
items.verification: Record payment using new composable; verify form behavior consistent.

items.title: Audit and Standardize Payment Email Templates
items.description: Review all 5 payment email templates for consistent branding, footer, and unsubscribe link.
steps: Audit: PaymentReceived, OverpaymentNotification, PaymentVerificationApproved, PaymentVerificationRejected, InvoiceReminder
Create shared email layout component
Standardize: header, footer, colors, fonts
Add unsubscribe link to all emails
Test all emails render correctly
items.verification: Send each email type; verify consistent appearance.

items.title: Review and Enhance Payment Cache Strategy
items.description: Audit PaymentObserver cache invalidation. Implement tagged caching and cache warming.
steps: Audit current cache keys in PaymentObserver and FinanceCacheService
Implement tagged caching: Cache::tags(['payments', 'landlord:'.$id])
Add cache warming after payment to reduce first-hit latency
Document cache strategy in docs/adr/
Add cache metrics logging
items.verification: Record payment; immediately view dashboard; verify data fresh.

items.title: Implement Hybrid Real-Time Updates
items.description: Use WebSocket for recent payments, polling for historical stats. Reduces server load.
steps: Keep PaymentReceived WebSocket broadcast for real-time notifications
Add polling endpoint for dashboard stats (30 second interval)
Implement smart refresh: WebSocket triggers immediate stat refresh
Add connection status indicator to dashboard
Fall back to polling if WebSocket disconnected
acceptance_criteria: Fallback to polling when WebSocket unavailable
items.verification: Disconnect WebSocket; verify polling takes over; reconnect; verify WebSocket resumes.

items.title: Replace Hardcoded KES in Email Blade Templates
items.description: 13 email blade templates hardcode 'KES' or 'KSh' in currency formatting. Update to use dynamic currency from the related model (invoice, payment, lease).
steps: Audit all email blade files for hardcoded KES/KSh references
Update each Mailable class to pass currency_symbol and currency_code from the related model
Replace hardcoded 'KES'/'KSh' in blade views with {{ $currency_symbol }} / {{ $currency_code }}
Test each email template renders correctly with KES and USD
acceptance_criteria: Zero hardcoded KES/KSh in email templates
Currency symbol passed from Mailable to blade
Emails render correctly for all 4 supported currencies

items.title: Replace Hardcoded KES in PDF, Report, and Export Templates
items.description: Tracer bullet audit found 57+ hardcoded KES instances across 37 files: 11 blade templates (PDF/report/export), 13 Maatwebsite Excel export classes, 5 CSV heading methods, ReportsController CSV generator, and InvoiceController flash messages. All must use dynamic currency from Currency enum.
steps: Audit PDF, report, export blade files AND Maatwebsite Excel export classes for hardcoded KES/KSh
Update service classes (FinanceExportService, ReportsController, TenantController, InvoiceController) to pass currency
Update 13 Maatwebsite Excel export classes to accept currencyCode constructor param
Replace hardcoded values in 11 blade templates
Update export class callers in controllers to pass currency code
Test PDF, XLSX, and CSV generation with non-KES currencies
E2E browser verification of exported documents
acceptance_criteria: Zero hardcoded KES/KSh in PDF/report/export blade templates
Zero hardcoded KES in Maatwebsite Excel export headings
Currency passed from service layer to all templates and export classes
PDFs render correctly with non-KES currencies
CSV and XLSX exports use correct currency headers
InvoiceController flash message uses dynamic currency
definition_of_done: CSV heading methods use dynamic currency
items.verification: Generate PDF invoice with USD currency; verify '$' symbol appears instead of 'KSh'. grep -r KES on target files returns 0 matches.

items.title: Replace Hardcoded KES in Vue Frontend Components
items.description: 20+ Vue files have hardcoded 'KES' or 'KSh' in form labels, input prefixes, and display values. Replace with dynamic currency from page props or useFormatters composable.
steps: Audit all Vue files for hardcoded KES/KSh strings
Ensure useFormatters composable supports currency parameter
Update form input prefixes: static 'KES' → dynamic {{ currencyCode }}
Update display labels: '(KES)' → '(' + currencyCode + ')'
Pass currency from controller page props where not already present
acceptance_criteria: Zero hardcoded KES/KSh in Vue components
useFormatters currency-aware for all formatting calls
Form labels use dynamic currency code
Input prefixes use dynamic currency symbol
items.verification: grep -r 'KES\|KSh' resources/js/ returns 0 hardcoded hits (excluding enum definitions).

items.title: Replace Hardcoded KES in PHP Service Classes
items.description: 15+ PHP service files hardcode 'KES' or 'KSh' in notification messages, CSV headers, result messages, and QR codes. Replace with dynamic currency from model relationships.
steps: Audit PHP service files for hardcoded KES/KSh strings
Update NotificationService message templates to read currency from invoice/lease
Update TemplateService template strings
Update FinanceExportService CSV headers to accept currency parameter
Update PaymentResult classes to read currency from payment model
Update remaining services (SchedulerService, PaymentQrCodeService, RefundService, etc.)
acceptance_criteria: Zero hardcoded KES/KSh in PHP service files
Currency read from model relationships (invoice->currency, payment->currency, lease->unit->building currency)
Notification messages use correct currency symbol
items.verification: grep -r 'KES\|KSh' app/Services/ returns 0 hardcoded hits (excluding enum/config definitions).

metrics: Multi-currency payments work (KES, USD, EUR, GBP)
Daily reconciliation runs with alerts on discrepancies
Tiered platform fees applied based on volume
Offline payments queue and process with SMS confirmation
All payment Vue components use shared composable
